title: Sphaeropteris celebica
intro: Sphaeropteris celebica, also known as Celebes cycad, is a species of plant in the family Cyatheaceae, characterized by its palm-like appearance and unique, cycad-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeropteris celebica include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sphaeropteris celebica is often prepared into a decoction or infused in tea to treat various health ailments, as well as being used in the form of a topical paste for skin conditions.
taxonomy: Sphaeropteris celebica is classified within the division Pteridophyta and the class Polypodiopsida. This species belongs to the family Cyathea and is further categorized under the subfamily Cyatheoideae. The genus Sphaeropteris is part of the family Cyathea. Within the genus Sphaeropteris, Sphaeropteris celebica is one of the species that have been identified. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and distinct characteristics of Sphaeropteris celebica.
